title: WingData
description: Discovery of a Wing FTP Server (v7.4.3) on the client portal revealed a vulnerability to CVE-2025-47812, an unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE) via a NULL-byte authentication bypass. Exploitation yielded a reverse shell, allowing for the enumeration of the application's data directory where a configuration file and a user database were found. Analysis of settings.xml and wacky.xml provided a salted password hash for the user wacky, which was cracked and used to establish an SSH connection.

title: TwoMillion
description: Analysis of minified JS revealed an API for invite code generation, yielding a Base64-encoded string via a POST request. After registration, API enumeration allowed for administrative privilege escalation. OS Command Injection in the username parameter yielded a reverse shell. System was found vulnerable to CVE-2023-0386 (OverlayFS), granting root via SUID manipulation.

title: Cap
description: Exposed IDOR vulnerability in the web dashboard allowed for the download of other users' network captures. Analysis of a PCAP file revealed cleartext FTP credentials. Local enumeration showed Python 3.8 configured with dangerous capabilities (cap_setuid), allowing immediate privilege escalation to root.

title: Expressway
description: Exposed IKE port allowed for aggressive mode sniffing, leading to the crack of a Pre-Shared Key (PSK). This provided access to a VPN tunnel where internal services were reachable. After pivoting, a cleartext password was found in a configuration file, granting SSH access. Final privilege escalation was achieved by exploiting a misconfigured SUID binary.
